stories.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
stories.factualSummary: CISA said it lacked a ready response plan when a contractor exposure surfaced and had to assemble its incident playbook during the early stages of the response.
takeaways: Incident response breaks down quickly when playbooks do not exist for the exact scenario.
The report is a reminder that government security teams still need rehearsed, written procedures before a crisis starts.

stories.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
stories.factualSummary: Meta pulled a feature that let people generate AI images from public Instagram accounts after backlash over consent, privacy, and notification gaps.
takeaways: Opt-out controls were not enough to make the feature acceptable once public criticism intensified.
Consumer AI features built on social identity data are moving faster than most users can evaluate their privacy impact.

stories.title: Bluesky's interim CEO, Toni Schneider, drops the 'interim'
stories.factualSummary: Toni Schneider moved from interim to permanent CEO at Bluesky and said he wants to emphasize smaller spaces and more private communities.
takeaways: The company is signaling leadership continuity after a short transition period.
Bluesky appears to be leaning into private-community features rather than pure scale chasing.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
stories.factualSummary: Apple filed suit against OpenAI alleging trade secret theft and breach of contract, pushing the companies' AI relationship into a legal dispute.
takeaways: A major platform-vs-model conflict now has a courtroom dimension.
The case could affect how tightly major device and AI ecosystems stay coupled.

stories.title: Disney+ is considering a free streaming tier, report says
stories.factualSummary: Disney+ is reportedly weighing a free tier with selected content as streaming services keep looking for ways to compete with ad-supported alternatives.
takeaways: Free or lightly monetized tiers remain an important growth lever for streaming platforms.
The exact catalog and launch timing still appear undecided.

stories.title: Five ways to use YouTube Shorts to grow your channel
stories.factualSummary: YouTube outlines interactive stickers, polls, Q&A, add-yours prompts, and video replies as ways to turn Shorts viewers into a community.
takeaways: The platform is pushing creators toward engagement loops rather than simple reach.
Audience participation features are becoming a core growth tactic for Shorts.

stories.title: Building for families in Turkiye and MENA with new kid accounts
stories.factualSummary: YouTube introduced supervised kid accounts in Turkiye and MENA with parent-led settings, default protections, and a Shorts feed timer.
takeaways: Family safety controls are becoming more region-specific and more configurable.
Short-form consumption is now part of the parental-control design surface.

stories.title: How to watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup on YouTube
stories.factualSummary: YouTube mapped out how fans can follow the 2026 FIFA World Cup through official broadcasters, live coverage, Shorts, highlights, and creator content.
takeaways: YouTube is using the tournament to reinforce its role as both a live-sports and highlights platform.
Creators and official media partners are being treated as complementary distribution layers.

stories.title: Anthropic's browser agent got hijacked 31.5% of the time before safeguards engaged
stories.factualSummary: VentureBeat compared frontier prompt-injection disclosures and found that Anthropic's browser agent still had a high attack success rate before safeguards were applied.
takeaways: Browser-facing agents remain one of the most exposed surfaces for prompt injection.
Vendor disclosure formats still make apples-to-apples security comparison difficult.

stories.title: MeMo's memory model lets teams upgrade their LLM without retraining it - and performance jumps 26%
stories.factualSummary: Researchers behind MeMo separate memory from reasoning so teams can add knowledge without full retraining, then swap reasoning models without rebuilding the memory layer.
takeaways: The architecture points to a modular alternative to full retraining or heavy RAG stacks.
Memory and reasoning can evolve on different schedules if the interface stays stable.

stories.title: Pinterest cut AI costs 90% by gutting a frontier model's vision layer
stories.factualSummary: Pinterest cut image-recommendation costs by replacing a frontier model's vision encoder with proprietary embeddings and tuning for its own discovery graph.
takeaways: Model efficiency gains can come from architecture changes rather than a brand-new model family.
Custom embeddings can be more valuable than raw model size when the use case is highly specialized.

stories.title: Mistral AI launches Vibe, expands into industrial AI and announces data center push to challenge OpenAI
stories.factualSummary: Mistral used its conference to announce an industrial-AI expansion, a new data center push, and a rebranding of its consumer assistant as it targets enterprise customers.
takeaways: Infrastructure ownership remains central to enterprise AI positioning.
The company is broadening beyond model releases into full-stack deployment and industrial use cases.

stories.title: How DeepSeek's radical architecture is shattering Silicon Valley's token moat
stories.factualSummary: DeepSeek's permanent price cuts and cache-focused architecture are pressuring frontier-model economics and undercutting Western token pricing.
takeaways: Infrastructure efficiency is now a major competitive lever in model markets.
Token pricing pressure can reshape the economics of enterprise AI adoption.
